headline: Chip stocks start Q3 with a dud after record Q2 rallies
detail: After a massive run, semis are pulling back right at the Q3 open. This directly hits SMH, SOXL, and your broader chip concentration. On margin, a sector-wide cooling period is more painful than it looks on paper - small moves get amplified.
rec: No new buys in semis right now. Watch SMH's $600 level - if it holds, SOXL stop at $200 may be fine to keep; if SMH breaks down, consider tightening SOXL's stop above $200 to limit further damage. Ideas to consider, not advice.

headline: Meta pops 9% on cloud AI compute push
detail: Meta monetizing excess AI compute capacity is a real positive signal for AI infrastructure demand broadly. It doesn't directly move your chip names today, but it keeps the AI capex narrative alive, which is the longer-term bull case for semis and names like SMH and CRWD.
rec: No action needed today - you don't hold Meta. Monitor whether this lifts sentiment back into semis and SMH specifically. If SMH stabilizes, that's a reason to hold rather than cut. Not a reason to add yet.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: This is more noise than signal for your book - it's an inter-AI-company spat with no direct impact on chip names or your holdings. It does keep the AI narrative in the news cycle, which keeps your sector in focus.
rec: Nothing to trade here. Your holdings aren't Palantir, OpenAI, or Anthropic, and this headline doesn't change the demand picture for semis or software names you hold. Watch and ignore.

headline: CRWD holds at +7.4% - PANW scanner setup active
detail: CRWD is your strongest performer and cybersecurity is holding up better than pure semis. PANW has the top scanner grade today - Accumulate 9/12, Grade A daily/4H/1H alignment - and cybersecurity peers tend to move together.
rec: PANW scanner entry is 351.34-351.69, stop 350.62 - tight and clean. If you want cybersecurity exposure to complement CRWD, this is the only setup today with a real Grade A pattern. Small size only given margin load. Ideas to consider, not advice.
